intent: Kernel module load/unload auditing (catalog #27): -a always,exit -S init_module -S finit_module -S delete_module (b64+b32 paired) on el10 (audit 4.0.3). LOW-MEDIUM -- modules load mostly at boot, occasionally at runtime.

rules.line: -a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S init_module -S finit_module -S delete_module -k modules
-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S init_module -S finit_module -S delete_module -k modules

suspected_trap: These syscall rules are LOW-MEDIUM (module load/unload is mostly boot-time), NOT HIGH despite being -S syscall rules (f3 3.5 -- a naive 'any syscall = HIGH' impl over-warns). The b64/b32 pair is intentional 32+64-bit coverage (catalog #30 arch-paired), NOT a redundant duplicate -- do not collapse the two rules into one or count them as redundant. 1200 B/event (syscall-class).
